programmes.show_description: Not For A Drink, A ****, Or A G*****n Prayer: A guilty Mai Ling tries to make things right with a vengeful Ah Sahm. Penny finds her business in trouble. (S2, ep 5)
Series shining a new light on modern policing in Manchester and the challenges faced by officers as they come face to face with a range of criminal and social issues. (S1, ep 9)
Wales: Adrenaline-fuelled documentary following Britain's police as they wage war against crime. This time, cameras chart the admirable efforts of police teams from Wales. (S1, ep 1)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S11, ep 1)

At Detroit Metro Airport, a traveller minding manners may need to mind his story instead and the Chicago canine unit knows smugglers don't play games. (S1, ep 14)

The Circle: Relieved of her post and exiled back to Bajor, Kira helps to reveal the hidden force behind The Circle, and a greater secret that could destroy them all.
Crystal Skull: When Daniel seemingly vanishes during an exploration, SG-1 must seek out the assistance of his eccentric grandfather. (S3, ep 21)
Shattered: Chakotay explores Voyager's history, quite literally, when his body is left in a state of temporal flux. (S7, ep 11)
Code of Honor: Tasha finds herself in danger when the Enterprise travels to the planet Ligon II to negotiate a treaty for the use of a rare vaccine.
Dragon Tales: A dinosaur comes through an anomaly, followed by a medieval knight who believes it is a dragon. The team must stop both from rampaging through London. (S3, ep 7)
Mama Bear: A former inmate firefighter, harbouring a fierce grudge against Sharon, returns to town to carry out his revenge. (S1, ep 11)
Black Penguin: The Fly Team and Jaeger head to Berlin when the 19-year-old son of an American billionaire is found unresponsive in his apartment. (S1, ep 20)
Blood Magic: As Eve and Adelind investigate the mirrors, Nick makes a new discovery about the Wesen community after a murder at a nursing home. (S6, ep 10)
To A Man With A Hammer, Everything Looks Like A Nail: Ah Sahm, Young Jun and Hong head to a border town owned by the host of a lucrative fight tournament. (S2, ep 6)
Series shining a new light on modern policing in Manchester and the challenges faced by officers as they come face to face with a range of criminal and social issues. (S1, ep 10)
Devon/Cornwall: Adrenaline-fuelled documentary following Britain's police as they wage war against crime. This week's fix focuses on police in Devon and Cornwall. (S1, ep 3)

Airside at JFK Airport, officers rush to inspect some high-maintenance passengers and a mother and son are suspected of money structuring in Detroit. (S1, ep 7)

The Siege: While Sisko leads a daring last stand against the Bajoran take-over forces, Kira and Dax embark on a desperate mission to reveal the truth about the coup.
Nemesis: SG-1 face their most formidable foe yet, a creature of mass destruction that not even the Asgard can control. (S3, ep 22)
Lineage: Tom and B'Elanna are overjoyed to learn that they are going to have a baby, but the latter's concerns soon start to grow. (S7, ep 12)
Haven: Counsellor Deanna Troi is caught between her feelings for Riker and her devotion to family customs when she faces a pre-arranged marriage.
Oh, Brother: Giant ant-like creatures from the future come through an anomaly. Abby's brother Jack ends up stranded in the future and the team go in to rescue him. (S3, ep 8)
Day of Death: The team try to get Rosalind to help as they frantically search for Officer Chen. Officer Lopez finds Wesley in a bad way. (S2, ep 11)
Now and Then: Harper takes Lucy under her wing as she returns to duty after her abduction. Nolan invites his son's fiance on a ride-along. (S2, ep 12)
Where the Wild Things Were: As Eve travels through the mirror to take on a mysterious foe, Nick attempts to follow her. (S6, ep 11)
